nodes.context: Outlaw motorcycle gang based in Southern California, founded in 1959.
Cult led by Charles Manson, active in late 1960s.
Outlaw motorcycle club with a chapter in Glendale.
Outlaw motorcycle club with a chapter in San Bernardino.

edges.evidence_quote: The Straight Satans are perhaps most infamous for their connection to the Manson Family cult.
edges.context: The Straight Satans had a close association with the Manson Family, with members visiting Spahn Ranch and providing weapons.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 1972, members of several Southern California motorcycle gangs—including the Straight Satans, the Glendale chapter of the Mongols, the San Bernardino chapter of the Hells Angels, and Night Riders from Glendale—were implicated in the sexual assault of a 15-year-old girl.
edges.context: Members of the Straight Satans and Mongols were jointly implicated in a criminal incident.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 1972, members of several Southern California motorcycle gangs—including the Straight Satans, the Glendale chapter of the Mongols, the San Bernardino chapter of the Hells Angels, and Night Riders from Glendale—were implicated in the sexual assault of a 15-year-old girl.
edges.context: Members of the Straight Satans and Hells Angels were jointly implicated in a criminal incident.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 1972, members of several Southern California motorcycle gangs—including the Straight Satans, the Glendale chapter of the Mongols, the San Bernardino chapter of the Hells Angels, and Night Riders from Glendale—were implicated in the sexual assault of a 15-year-old girl.
edges.context: Members of the Straight Satans and Night Riders were jointly implicated in a criminal incident.
